H: How long can I leave a four year old home alone?

When considering leaving a four-year-old child home alone, it is vital to hold close the perspective of the least advantaged party—the child—who is most vulnerable and dependent at this tender age. Developmentally, children of four are not equipped to manage emergencies or make safe decisions independently, and thus, even brief periods alone can pose significant risks.

Historically, in many non-Western contexts—such as traditional African and Asian societies—childcare was a communal responsibility, woven into the fabric of extended families and communities. This collective care ensured that no child was left isolated, a practice that contrasts with some modern Western norms shaped by industrialization and urbanization.
